---
title: "Visualizations"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-25"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r cars}
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. Load libraries
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(openxlsx)
library(grid)    # for unit()

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. Read + prepare nationwide data (same as before)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
data_nationwide <- read.xlsx(
  "/Users/anaghchattopadhyay/Documents/Nilanjan Project/TMC_BCProject/Data/BC_Incidence_Rates.xlsx"
)

nationwide_plot <- data_nationwide %>%
  mutate(
    AgeMid = (age_start + age_end) / 2,
    # force “75–80” midpoint to exactly 80
    AgeMid = if_else(age_start == 75 & age_end == 80, 80, AgeMid),
    # replace 0 with 0.1 so log10 never sees a true zero
    rates_adj = if_else(rates == 0, 0.1, rates)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(AgeMid), !is.na(rates_adj))


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. Read + pivot state‐wise data
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
data <- read.csv(
  "/Users/anaghchattopadhyay/Documents/Nilanjan Project/TMC_BCProject/Data/BC_data_new.csv"
)

data_long <- data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -Registry..year.,
    names_to  = "AgeGroup",
    values_to = "Incidence"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    AgeGroup = gsub("^X", "", AgeGroup),
    AgeGroup = gsub("\\.", "-", AgeGroup),
    AgeMid = case_when(
      AgeGroup == "0-4"   ~ 2.5,
      AgeGroup == "5-9"   ~ 7.5,
      AgeGroup == "10-14" ~ 12.5,
      AgeGroup == "15-19" ~ 17.5,
      AgeGroup == "20-24" ~ 22.5,
      AgeGroup == "25-29" ~ 27.5,
      AgeGroup == "30-34" ~ 32.5,
      AgeGroup == "35-39" ~ 37.5,
      AgeGroup == "40-44" ~ 42.5,
      AgeGroup == "45-49" ~ 47.5,
      AgeGroup == "50-54" ~ 52.5,
      AgeGroup == "55-59" ~ 57.5,
      AgeGroup == "60-64" ~ 62.5,
      AgeGroup == "65-69" ~ 67.5,
      AgeGroup == "70-74" ~ 72.5,
      AgeGroup == "75-"  ~ 80,
      TRUE                ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Incidence_adj = if_else(Incidence == 0, 0.1, Incidence)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(AgeMid), !is.na(Incidence_adj))


# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4. Produce the plot with “log10 of the values” labeled on the y‐axis
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(data_long, aes(x = AgeMid, y = Incidence_adj, color = Registry..year.)) +
  
  # 4a) state/year‐specific curves
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1, na.rm = TRUE) +
  
  # 4b) nationwide reference (black dotted)
  geom_line(
    data        = nationwide_plot,
    aes(x = AgeMid, y = rates_adj),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color       = "black",
    linetype    = "dotted",
    linewidth   = 1,
    na.rm       = TRUE
  ) +
  
  # 4c) labels and title
  labs(
    title = "Breast Cancer Incidence by Age Group\n(log scale, axis shows log-values)",
    x     = "Age (years)",
    y     = "log (Incidence rate per 100,000)"
  ) +
  
  # 4d) theme tweeks
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position  = "right",
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title     = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size  = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.margin    = margin(l = 5),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray85"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE)) +
  
  # 4e) x‐axis from 0 to 80, no extra padding
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 80),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) + 
  # 4f) log10 transform on y but label ticks with log10(values)
  scale_y_continuous(
    trans  = "log10",
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000),
    labels = function(x) round(log10(x), 1)
  )

```



```{r}
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Assumptions:
# 1. `data_DOE` is your main data frame and has columns:
#      - ID
#      - DateOfInterview  (of class Date; if not, convert as shown)
# 2. `data_mmgy` is your mammography data frame and has column:
#      - ID  (matching the IDs in data_DOE)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# 1. Ensure DateOfInterview is of class Date. Adjust the format string if needed.
if (!inherits(data_DOE$DateOfInterview, "Date")) {
  data_DOE$DateOfInterview <- as.Date(
    data_DOE$DateOfInterview,
    format = "%Y-%m-%d"  # change if your dates use a different format
  )
}

# 2. Subset data_DOE to only those rows whose ID appears in data_mmgy$ID
doe_mmgy <- data_DOE[data_DOE$ID %in% data_mmgy$ID, ]

# 3. Create 100 equally‐spaced breaks spanning the full range of DateOfInterview
all_dates  <- data_DOE$DateOfInterview
breaks_100 <- seq(
  from = min(all_dates, na.rm = TRUE),
  to   = max(all_dates, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 101
)

# 4. Compute histogram objects without plotting
h_all  <- hist(all_dates,
               breaks = breaks_100,
               plot   = FALSE)
h_mmgy <- hist(doe_mmgy$DateOfInterview,
               breaks = breaks_100,
               plot   = FALSE)

# 5. Plot the "all interviews" histogram in light gray
plot(h_all,
     col    = "lightgray",
     border = "white",
     main   = "Histogram of DateOfInterview\n(All vs. Mammography linked)",
     xlab   = "Date of Interview",
     ylab   = "Count")

# 6. Overlay the "mammography‐linked" histogram in semi‐transparent red
plot(h_mmgy,
     col    = rgb(1, 0, 0, alpha = 0.5),  # red with 50% opacity
     border = NA,
     add    = TRUE)

# 7. Add a legend to distinguish the two distributions
legend("topright",
       legend = c("All interviews", "Mammography linked interviews"),
       fill   = c("lightgray", rgb(1, 0, 0, alpha = 0.5)),
       border = c("white", NA),
       bty    = "n")


```


```{r}
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Assumptions (same as before):
# 1. data_DOE has columns “ID” and “DateOfInterview” (of class Date).
# 2. data_mmgy has column “ID” (subset of data_DOE$ID).
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# 1. Coerce DateOfInterview to Date if needed
if (!inherits(data_DOE$DateOfInterview, "Date")) {
  data_DOE$DateOfInterview <- as.Date(
    data_DOE$DateOfInterview,
    format = "%Y-%m-%d"  # adjust if your dates use a different format
  )
}

# 2. Subset data_DOE to just those with mammography records
doe_mmgy <- data_DOE[data_DOE$ID %in% data_mmgy$ID, ]

# 3. Order those mammography‐linked rows by DateOfInterview (newest first)
ord_mmgy_desc <- order(doe_mmgy$DateOfInterview, decreasing = TRUE)

# 4. Take the 400 latest mammography cases as validation
val_mmgy      <- doe_mmgy[ord_mmgy_desc[1:400], ]

# 5. Grab their IDs
val_ids       <- val_mmgy$ID

# 6. Define the new validation_set (all rows of data_DOE whose ID is in val_ids)
validation_set <- data_DOE[data_DOE$ID %in% val_ids, ]

# 7. Define the new training_set (all other rows of data_DOE)
training_set   <- data_DOE[!data_DOE$ID %in% val_ids, ]

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Now recompute the counts/percentages “how many in each set have mammography?”
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# (2) Count total rows in each subset
n_val   <- nrow(validation_set)
n_train <- nrow(training_set)

# (3) Count how many of those have an ID in data_mmgy
#     (validation_set should all have mammography, but we'll compute anyway)
n_val_with_mmgy   <- sum(validation_set$ID %in% data_mmgy$ID, na.rm = TRUE)
n_train_with_mmgy <- sum(training_set$ID   %in% data_mmgy$ID, na.rm = TRUE)

# (4) Compute percentages
perc_val   <- n_val_with_mmgy   / n_val   * 100
perc_train <- n_train_with_mmgy / n_train * 100

# (5) Print out results
cat("Validation set:    ", n_val_with_mmgy, "of", n_val,
    "have mammography (",
    round(perc_val, 1), "% )\n", sep = " ")
cat("Training set:      ", n_train_with_mmgy, "of", n_train,
    "have mammography (",
    round(perc_train, 1), "% )\n", sep = " ")

```
